item.title: Two-stage ferroelastic transition of stishovite due to one-dimensional hydrogen defects
item.description:
Author(s): Junwei Li, Sensen Wu, Haiyang Xian, Catalin Popescu, Davide Comboni, Michael I. Hanfland, Hongliang Dong, Binbin Yue, Yanhao Lin, and Qingyang Hu

The effects of one-dimensional hydrogen on the ferroelastic transition and elasticity of stishovite are determined up to 52.5 GPa using x-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and first-principles calculations. In contrast to the well-studied hydrogen-free stishovite or that with hydrogarnetlike poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094111] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Junwei Li, Sensen Wu, Haiyang Xian, Catalin Popescu, Davide Comboni, Michael I. Hanfland, Hongliang Dong, Binbin Yue, Yanhao Lin, and Qingyang Hu

The effects of one-dimensional hydrogen on the ferroelastic transition and elasticity of stishovite are determined up to 52.5 GPa using x-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and first-principles calculations. In contrast to the well-studied hydrogen-free stishovite or that with hydrogarnetlike poin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094111] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-stage ferroelastic transition of stishovite due to one-dimensional hydrogen defects
item.dc:creator: Junwei Li, Sensen Wu, Haiyang Xian, Catalin Popescu, Davide Comboni, Michael I. Hanfland, Hongliang Dong, Binbin Yue, Yanhao Lin, and Qingyang Hu

item.title: Compact localized fermions and Ising anyons in a chiral spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Tim Bauer and Johannes Reuther

Quasiparticle hybridization remains a major challenge in realizing and controlling exotic states of matter in existing quantum simulation platforms. We report the absence of hybridization for compact localized states (CLSs) emerging in the chiral spin liquid described by the Yao-Kivelson model. The …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094442] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tim Bauer and Johannes Reuther

Quasiparticle hybridization remains a major challenge in realizing and controlling exotic states of matter in existing quantum simulation platforms. We report the absence of hybridization for compact localized states (CLSs) emerging in the chiral spin liquid described by the Yao-Kivelson model. The …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094442] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Compact localized fermions and Ising anyons in a chiral spin liquid

item.title: Linear magnetoresistance and anomalous Hall effect in the superconductor ${\mathrm{NiBi}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Sant'ana, Jully Paola Peña Pacheco, David Möckli, Fabiano Mesquita da Rosa, Sergio Garcia Magalhães, Andre Avelino Pasa, Paulo Pureur, and Milton Andre Tumelero

The ${\mathrm{NiBi}}_{3}$ compound exhibits a compelling interplay between superconductivity and magnetism, further enriched by topological characteristics that make it an exceptional platform for exploring emergent electronic phenomena. Here, we report experimental evidence of unconventional magnet…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094443] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Sant'ana, Jully Paola Peña Pacheco, David Möckli, Fabiano Mesquita da Rosa, Sergio Garcia Magalhães, Andre Avelino Pasa, Paulo Pureur, and Milton Andre Tumelero

The NiBi3 compound exhibits a compelling interplay between superconductivity and magnetism, further enriched by topological characteristics that make it an exceptional platform for exploring emergent electronic phenomena. Here, we report experimental evidence of unconventional magnetic phenomena in …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094443] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Linear magnetoresistance and anomalous Hall effect in the superconductor ${\mathrm{NiBi}}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Sant'ana, Jully Paola Peña Pacheco, David Möckli, Fabiano Mesquita da Rosa, Sergio Garcia Magalhães, Andre Avelino Pasa, Paulo Pureur, and Milton Andre Tumelero

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: From quantum geometry to nonlinear optics and gerbes: Recent advances in topological band theory
item.description:
Author(s): Tomáš Bzdušek

This Perspective explores recent advances in topological band theory, focusing on the quantum geometric tensor, delicate and multigap topology, and higher-form structures like bundle gerbes. It highlights how these geometric features relate to first- and higher-order optical responses, including the quantization of shift currents. By moving beyond conventional Berryology, these threads offer new insights into discovery and materials design for electronic and synthetic condensed matter systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099601] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tomáš Bzdušek

This Perspective explores recent advances in topological band theory, focusing on the quantum geometric tensor, delicate and multigap topology, and higher-form structures like bundle gerbes. It highlights how these geometric features relate to first- and higher-order optical responses, including the quantization of shift currents. By moving beyond conventional Berryology, these threads offer new insights into discovery and materials design for electronic and synthetic condensed matter systems.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099601] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: From quantum geometry to nonlinear optics and gerbes: Recent advances in topological band theory
item.dc:creator: Tomáš Bzdušek
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: High-pressure formation and characterization of a boron carbide polymorph featuring bent C–B–C chains
item.description:
Author(s): Akun Liang, Umbertoluca Ranieri, James Spender, Charles Lamb, Sarah Bolton, Bernhard Massani, Ryan Stewart McWilliams, Timofey Fedotenko, Konstantin Glazyrin, Nico Giordano, Jonathan Wright, Florian Trybel, and Dominique Laniel

Boron carbide is a material of choice for multiple industries, e.g., aerospace, as a lightweight structural ceramic due to its high hardness, high melting temperature, and low density. However, its mechanical properties have been observed to radically degrade under shockwave compression, presumably …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104108] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Akun Liang, Umbertoluca Ranieri, James Spender, Charles Lamb, Sarah Bolton, Bernhard Massani, Ryan Stewart McWilliams, Timofey Fedotenko, Konstantin Glazyrin, Nico Giordano, Jonathan Wright, Florian Trybel, and Dominique Laniel

Boron carbide is a material of choice for multiple industries, e.g., aerospace, as a lightweight structural ceramic due to its high hardness, high melting temperature, and low density. However, its mechanical properties have been observed to radically degrade under shockwave compression, presumably …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104108] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Unlocking doping effects on altermagnetism in MnTe: Emergence of quasi-altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Nayana Devaraj, Anumita Bose, Arindom Das, Md Afsar Reja, Arijit Mandal, Awadhesh Narayan, and B. R. K. Nanda

Governed by specific symmetries, altermagnetism is an emerging field in condensed matter physics, characterized by unique spin-splitting of the bands in the momentum space coexisting with the compensated magnetization as in antiferromagnets. As crystals can have tailored and unintended defects, it i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104438] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nayana Devaraj, Anumita Bose, Arindom Das, Md Afsar Reja, Arijit Mandal, Awadhesh Narayan, and B. R. K. Nanda

Governed by specific symmetries, altermagnetism is an emerging field in condensed matter physics, characterized by unique spin-splitting of the bands in the momentum space coexisting with the compensated magnetization as in antiferromagnets. As crystals can have tailored and unintended defects, it i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104438] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Ideal nodal-sphere semimetal in the three-dimensional boron allotrope ${\text{CT-B}}_{24}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Jing Gao, Yanfeng Ge, and Yan Gao

Nodal-sphere semimetals (NSSMs), featuring spherical band degeneracies in momentum space, constitute a fascinating class of topological materials. However, their realization in real materials is severely hampered by discrete crystallographic symmetry constraints, often resulting in gapped “pseudo” n…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115145] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Jing Gao, Yanfeng Ge, and Yan Gao

Nodal-sphere semimetals (NSSMs), featuring spherical band degeneracies in momentum space, constitute a fascinating class of topological materials. However, their realization in real materials is severely hampered by discrete crystallographic symmetry constraints, often resulting in gapped “pseudo” n…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115145] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Study of exciton-photon coupling in CuCl semiconductor microcavities with large decay of cavity photons
item.description:
Author(s): Rintaro Miyanaga, Goro Oohata, Satoshi Tanaka, and Kohji Mizoguchi

In CuCl semiconductor microcavities with large decay of the cavity photon (CP), we have studied the dependence of the coupling energy between excitons and CPs on the position of the active layer in the cavity layer, using angle-resolved transmission (ART) spectroscopy. The ART spectra show that the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115309] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rintaro Miyanaga, Goro Oohata, Satoshi Tanaka, and Kohji Mizoguchi

In CuCl semiconductor microcavities with large decay of the cavity photon (CP), we have studied the dependence of the coupling energy between excitons and CPs on the position of the active layer in the cavity layer, using angle-resolved transmission (ART) spectroscopy. The ART spectra show that the …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115309] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Interplay of Rashba and valley Zeeman splittings in weak localization of spin-orbit coupled graphene
item.description:
Author(s): L. E. Golub

Weak localization theory is developed for graphene heterostructures with transition metal dichalcogenides and topological insulators where the Rashba and valley Zeeman spin splittings of the energy spectrum are large enough. The anomalous magnetoresistance in low fields caused by weak localization i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125306] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Weak localization theory is developed for graphene heterostructures with transition metal dichalcogenides and topological insulators where the Rashba and valley Zeeman spin splittings of the energy spectrum are large enough. The anomalous magnetoresistance in low fields caused by weak localization i…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125306] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Universal classes of disorder scattering for the in-plane anomalous Hall effect
item.description:
Author(s): Guoao Yang, Tao Qin, and Jianhui Zhou

The in-plane anomalous Hall effect (IPAHE) with planar Hall current and magnetization/magnetic fields in various quantum materials has received increasing attention. Most of the current efforts are devoted to the intrinsic part due to the Berry curvature of electronic bands, however, how disorder sc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125426] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

Most of the current efforts are devoted to the intrinsic part due to the Berry curvature of electronic bands, however, how disorder sc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125426] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Chiral spin liquid and quantum phase transition in the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Stefan Divic, Tomohiro Soejima (副島智大), Valentin Crépel, Michael P. Zaletel, and Andrew Millis

Recent advances in moiré engineering motivate the study of lattice models of strongly correlated electrons subjected to substantial orbital magnetic flux. We analyze the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model at one-quarter flux quantum per plaquette and a density of one electron per site, wher…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121107] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Stefan Divic, Tomohiro Soejima (副島智大), Valentin Crépel, Michael P. Zaletel, and Andrew Millis

Recent advances in moiré engineering motivate the study of lattice models of strongly correlated electrons subjected to substantial orbital magnetic flux. We analyze the triangular-lattice Hofstadter-Hubbard model at one-quarter flux quantum per plaquette and a density of one electron per site, wher…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121107] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026

item.title: Quasi-one-dimensional Coulomb drag between spin-polarized quantum wires
item.description:
Author(s): Mingyang Zheng, Rebika Makaju, Rasul Gazizulin, Alex Levchenko, Sadhvikas J. Addamane, and Dominique Laroche

Here, the authors present one-dimensional Coulomb drag measurements in the spin-polarized regime, achieved by applying a large magnetic field along the axis of the quantum wire. The temperature dependence of the drag is well described by a power law beyond a crossover temperature. Despite the presence of both reciprocal and nonreciprocal drag signals, the authors demonstrate a scaling of the power law exponent between the spinful and spin-polarized regimes, in agreement with predictions for clean ballistic quantum wires in the reciprocal regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121408] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mingyang Zheng, Rebika Makaju, Rasul Gazizulin, Alex Levchenko, Sadhvikas J. Addamane, and Dominique Laroche

Here, the authors present one-dimensional Coulomb drag measurements in the spin-polarized regime, achieved by applying a large magnetic field along the axis of the quantum wire. The temperature dependence of the drag is well described by a power law beyond a crossover temperature. Despite the presence of both reciprocal and nonreciprocal drag signals, the authors demonstrate a scaling of the power law exponent between the spinful and spin-polarized regimes, in agreement with predictions for clean ballistic quantum wires in the reciprocal regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121408] Published Fri Mar 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Quasi-one-dimensional Coulomb drag between spin-polarized quantum wires
item.dc:creator: Mingyang Zheng, Rebika Makaju, Rasul Gazizulin, Alex Levchenko, Sadhvikas J. Addamane, and Dominique Laroche

item.title: Scanning tunneling microscopy study of helimagnetic monolayer ${\mathrm{CrBr}}_{2}$ on the $s$-wave superconductor ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$: A topologically trivial system due to weak interfacial coupling
item.description:
Author(s): Yuanji Li, Ruotong Yin, Mingzhe Li, Shiyuan Wang, Jiashuo Gong, Ziyuan Chen, Jiakang Zhang, Dong-Lai Feng, and Ya-Jun Yan

Hybrid magnet-superconductor heterostructures attract significant interest for their potential to host unconventional superconductivity, topological superconductivity, and Majorana physics. Transition-metal dihalides ($\mathrm{M}{X}_{2}, \mathrm{M}=\mathrm{transition}$ metal, $X=\mathrm{Cl}$, Br, I)…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094519] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuanji Li, Ruotong Yin, Mingzhe Li, Shiyuan Wang, Jiashuo Gong, Ziyuan Chen, Jiakang Zhang, Dong-Lai Feng, and Ya-Jun Yan

Hybrid magnet-superconductor heterostructures attract significant interest for their potential to host unconventional superconductivity, topological superconductivity, and Majorana physics. Transition-metal dihalides (MX2, M=transition metal, X=Cl, Br, I) are compelling magnetic candidates due to th…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094519] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Scanning tunneling microscopy study of helimagnetic monolayer ${\mathrm{CrBr}}_{2}$ on the $s$-wave superconductor ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$: A topologically trivial system due to weak interfacial coupling

item.title: Singularities at load-induced moiré locking transitions in twist grain boundaries
item.description:
Author(s): Jin Wang and Erio Tosatti

Recent simulation work on the moiré formed by small angle crystalline twist grain boundaries (TGBs) revealed an unexpected load-induced first-order structural transition. Most likely universal, this unconventional transition drives a frictionally superlubric-to-locked transformation. It involves moi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104107] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jin Wang and Erio Tosatti

Recent simulation work on the moiré formed by small angle crystalline twist grain boundaries (TGBs) revealed an unexpected load-induced first-order structural transition. Most likely universal, this unconventional transition drives a frictionally superlubric-to-locked transformation. It involves moi…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104107] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Incommensurate twisted bilayer graphene: Emerging quasiperiodicity and stability
item.description:
Author(s): Ian Jauslin and Vieri Mastropietro

We consider a lattice model of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) for incommensurate twist angles, focusing on the role of large-momentum-transfer umklapp terms. These terms, which nearly connect the Fermi points of different layers, are typically neglected in effective continuum descriptions but could,…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115142] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

Author(s): Ian Jauslin and Vieri Mastropietro

We consider a lattice model of twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) for incommensurate twist angles, focusing on the role of large-momentum-transfer umklapp terms. These terms, which nearly connect the Fermi points of different layers, are typically neglected in effective continuum descriptions but could,…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115142] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.title: Theoretical study of the origin of x-ray atomic background anomalies by a full-potential multiple scattering approach
item.description:
Author(s): Kazuki Yoshikawa, Fabio Iesari, Yoshiaki Tamura, Naoki Nakatani, Fukiko Ota, Andrea Di Cicco, and Keisuke Hatada

Previous extended x-ray absorption fine structure (EXAFS) studies have shown evidence of anomalous contributions in the atomic background function whose origin was debated in the literature. The present study presents advanced multiple scattering calculations aimed to put to a test the existence and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115143] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

Previous extended x-ray absorption fine structure (EXAFS) studies have shown evidence of anomalous contributions in the atomic background function whose origin was debated in the literature. The present study presents advanced multiple scattering calculations aimed to put to a test the existence and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115143] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026

item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations in two-dimensional hole gases with competing cyclotron and Zeeman energy
item.description:
Author(s): Davide Costa, Lucas E. A. Stehouwer, Davide Degli Esposti, and Giordano Scappucci

Evaluating fundamental properties of two-dimensional hole gases in strained germanium quantum wells is a complex task due to the competition between cyclotron and Zeeman energies. Here, the authors present a self-consistent framework to accurately extract the effective mass, g factor, and quantum lifetime from Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations at a single temperature. By applying this procedure to a low-disorder Ge/SiGe heterostructure, the authors demonstrate a benchmark quantum mobility for holes in group-IV semiconductors, with clean fractional quantum Hall states observed.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115418] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Davide Costa, Lucas E. A. Stehouwer, Davide Degli Esposti, and Giordano Scappucci

Evaluating fundamental properties of two-dimensional hole gases in strained germanium quantum wells is a complex task due to the competition between cyclotron and Zeeman energies. Here, the authors present a self-consistent framework to accurately extract the effective mass, g factor, and quantum lifetime from Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations at a single temperature. By applying this procedure to a low-disorder Ge/SiGe heterostructure, the authors demonstrate a benchmark quantum mobility for holes in group-IV semiconductors, with clean fractional quantum Hall states observed.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115418] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations in two-dimensional hole gases with competing cyclotron and Zeeman energy
item.dc:creator: Davide Costa, Lucas E. A. Stehouwer, Davide Degli Esposti, and Giordano Scappucci
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect beyond Bloch geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Raffaele Resta

The intrinsic Hall effect, both linear and nonlinear, is a perspicuous manifestation of quantum geometry. In the current literature, the formulation is based on Bloch geometry, addressing crystalline systems in a mean-field framework. Nonetheless, the effect is a fundamental geometric response of a generic many-body ground state, not a band structure peculiarity. The author provides here an higher-level quantum-geometrical theory of the quadratic Hall effect, cast in very compact notations, and encompassing the known Bloch formulation as a special case.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125136] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Raffaele Resta

The intrinsic Hall effect, both linear and nonlinear, is a perspicuous manifestation of quantum geometry. In the current literature, the formulation is based on Bloch geometry, addressing crystalline systems in a mean-field framework. Nonetheless, the effect is a fundamental geometric response of a generic many-body ground state, not a band structure peculiarity. The author provides here an higher-level quantum-geometrical theory of the quadratic Hall effect, cast in very compact notations, and encompassing the known Bloch formulation as a special case.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125136] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Intrinsic nonlinear Hall effect beyond Bloch geometry
item.dc:creator: Raffaele Resta
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Robust semiclassical magnetization plateau in the kagome lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Capelo and Eric C. Andrade

Inspired by recent observations of the $1/3$ magnetization plateau in kagome-based magnets, we investigate the ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg model on the kagome lattice under the influence of an external magnetic field. Although the classical ground state at zero field depends on the sign of $…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125137] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gabriel Capelo and Eric C. Andrade

Inspired by recent observations of the 1/3 magnetization plateau in kagome-based magnets, we investigate the J1−J2 Heisenberg model on the kagome lattice under the influence of an external magnetic field. Although the classical ground state at zero field depends on the sign of J2, we find a robust 1…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125137] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Robust semiclassical magnetization plateau in the kagome lattice
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Capelo and Eric C. Andrade
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Tailoring corner states and exceptional points in altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou

Altermagnets (AMs) exhibit vanishing net magnetization but strong momentum-dependent spin splitting enforced by crystal symmetry. Here, we explore the non-Hermitian (NH) effects in dissipative two-dimensional AMs. We show that symmetry-compliant dissipation naturally induces an imaginary staggered e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100409] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou

Altermagnets (AMs) exhibit vanishing net magnetization but strong momentum-dependent spin splitting enforced by crystal symmetry. Here, we explore the non-Hermitian (NH) effects in dissipative two-dimensional AMs. We show that symmetry-compliant dissipation naturally induces an imaginary staggered e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100409] Published Thu Mar 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Tailoring corner states and exceptional points in altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Xiao-Ming Zhao, Cui-Xian Guo, Xin-Ran Ma, Xiao-Ran Wang, and Su-Peng Kou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Interlayer sliding and two-stage superconductivity in pressurized $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{b}}_{0.6}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{e}}_{0.4}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang

Here, the authors report a pressure-driven interlayer sliding in Nb0.6Re0.4Se2 that induces an unusual two-stage superconductivity. Synchrotron x-ray diffraction and Raman spectroscopy reveal an isosymmetric 2Hc-2Ha structural transition accompanied by phonon softening. Superconductivity emerges near the critical pressure Pc and develops a nonmonotonic phase diagram, characterized by a prominent superconducting dome followed by a slow reentrant enhancement at higher pressures. The findings highlight interlayer sliding as an effective route to engineer complex superconducting states in layered materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094516] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang

Here, the authors report a pressure-driven interlayer sliding in Nb0.6Re0.4Se2 that induces an unusual two-stage superconductivity. Synchrotron x-ray diffraction and Raman spectroscopy reveal an isosymmetric 2Hc-2Ha structural transition accompanied by phonon softening. Superconductivity emerges near the critical pressure Pc and develops a nonmonotonic phase diagram, characterized by a prominent superconducting dome followed by a slow reentrant enhancement at higher pressures. The findings highlight interlayer sliding as an effective route to engineer complex superconducting states in layered materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094516] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Interlayer sliding and two-stage superconductivity in pressurized $\mathrm{N}{\mathrm{b}}_{0.6}\mathrm{R}{\mathrm{e}}_{0.4}\mathrm{S}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Can Tian, Chenchen Liu, Wuhao Chen, Yuchen Zhang, Yuqiang Fang, and Xiaoli Huang

item.title: Hybrid collective excitations in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov

We develop a linear response theory for the dynamical proximity effect in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator (TS/FI) hybrids. Our approach integrates the nonequilibrium quasiclassical Keldysh-Usadel formalism for the TS with the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation for the FI's magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104514] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Karabassov, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, A. S. Vasenko, and A. A. Golubov

We develop a linear response theory for the dynamical proximity effect in topological superconductor/ferromagnetic insulator (TS/FI) hybrids. Our approach integrates the nonequilibrium quasiclassical Keldysh-Usadel formalism for the TS with the Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert equation for the FI's magnetiza…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104514] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Antiferromagnetism and Kekulé valence bond order in the honeycomb optical Su-Schrieffer-Heeger-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Sohan Malkaruge Costa, Benjamin Cohen-Stead, and Steven Johnston

The precise role of electron-phonon $(e\text{−}\mathrm{ph})$ coupling in graphene and related materials on a honeycomb lattice is not yet fully understood, despite extensive research on these systems. Here, we perform sign-problem-free determinant quantum Monte Carlo simulations of the optical Su-Sc…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115138] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sohan Malkaruge Costa, Benjamin Cohen-Stead, and Steven Johnston

The precise role of electron-phonon (e−ph) coupling in graphene and related materials on a honeycomb lattice is not yet fully understood, despite extensive research on these systems. Here, we perform sign-problem-free determinant quantum Monte Carlo simulations of the optical Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115138] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Generalized Cutler-Mott relation in a crossover between Fermi and non-Fermi liquid regimes of two-site charge Kondo simulators
item.description:
Author(s): T. K. T. Nguyen and M. N. Kiselev

We analyze the validity of the Cutler-Mott relations outside the Landau Fermi-liquid concept. We consider a two-site charge Kondo circuit as a paradigmatic example of a system possessing both Fermi- and non-Fermi- liquid properties. It is shown that the generalized Cutler-Mott-like relations derived…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115140] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. K. T. Nguyen and M. N. Kiselev

We analyze the validity of the Cutler-Mott relations outside the Landau Fermi-liquid concept. We consider a two-site charge Kondo circuit as a paradigmatic example of a system possessing both Fermi- and non-Fermi- liquid properties. It is shown that the generalized Cutler-Mott-like relations derived…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115140] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Strong nonreciprocal hyperbolic metamaterials mimicked by Weyl semimetal–dielectric multilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Kwang-Hyon Kim and Yong-Hyon Choe

Nonreciprocity has received much attention recently in the photonics community. However, nonreciprocal hyperbolicity has rarely been investigated in spite of its significance, and it has been limited to the perturbative regime in magneto-optical metamaterials. This work reports strong nonreciprocal …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125424] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

Author(s): Kwang-Hyon Kim and Yong-Hyon Choe

Nonreciprocity has received much attention recently in the photonics community. However, nonreciprocal hyperbolicity has rarely been investigated in spite of its significance, and it has been limited to the perturbative regime in magneto-optical metamaterials. This work reports strong nonreciprocal …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125424] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026

item.title: Engineering chiral-induced spin selectivity in an artificial topological quantum well
item.description:
Author(s): Lizhou Liu, Peng-Yi Liu, Tian-Yi Zhang, and Qing-Feng Sun

Chiral-induced spin selectivity (CISS) is a striking phenomenon in which spin-unpolarized electrons become spin polarized after traversing a chiral medium. Theoretical studies have shown that spin-orbit coupling, geometric chirality, and dephasing act cooperatively for this effect to emerge. Inspire…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121407] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lizhou Liu, Peng-Yi Liu, Tian-Yi Zhang, and Qing-Feng Sun

Chiral-induced spin selectivity (CISS) is a striking phenomenon in which spin-unpolarized electrons become spin polarized after traversing a chiral medium. Theoretical studies have shown that spin-orbit coupling, geometric chirality, and dephasing act cooperatively for this effect to emerge. Inspire…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121407] Published Wed Mar 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering chiral-induced spin selectivity in an artificial topological quantum well
item.dc:creator: Lizhou Liu, Peng-Yi Liu, Tian-Yi Zhang, and Qing-Feng Sun

item.title: Interacting ground states of moiré ladders
item.description:
Author(s): Paban Kumar Patra, Ranjith R. Kumar, Yixuan Huang, and Hridis K. Pal

Moiré materials have emerged as a rich platform for exploring strong correlation effects in low dimensions, with twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) as a paradigmatic example. To distill the essential ingredients driving moiré-induced phases, a simplified one-dimensional analog—a two-leg ladder with spat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104432] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

Moiré materials have emerged as a rich platform for exploring strong correlation effects in low dimensions, with twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) as a paradigmatic example. To distill the essential ingredients driving moiré-induced phases, a simplified one-dimensional analog—a two-leg ladder with spat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104432] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor and nonlinear electrical response
item.description:
Author(s): Kai Chen and Jie Zhu

We demonstrate that the non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor (QGT) governs nonlinear electrical responses in systems with a spectral line gap. The quantum metric, which is the symmetric component of the QGT and takes complex values in non-Hermitian systems, generates an intrinsic nonlinear conduct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115135] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

We demonstrate that the non-Hermitian quantum geometric tensor (QGT) governs nonlinear electrical responses in systems with a spectral line gap. The quantum metric, which is the symmetric component of the QGT and takes complex values in non-Hermitian systems, generates an intrinsic nonlinear conduct…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115135] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.title: Finite-size effects on metallization versus chiral Majorana fermions
item.description:
Author(s): Xin Yue (岳鑫), Guo-Jian Qiao (乔国健), and C. P. Sun (孙昌璞)

The search for chiral Majorana fermions in quantum anomalous Hall insulator/$s$-wave superconductor heterostructures has attracted intense interest, yet remains controversial due to the lack of conclusive evidence. A key issue is that the heterostructure's metallization can produce half-integer cond…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115416] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

A key issue is that the heterostructure's metallization can produce half-integer conduc…

item.title: Ultrafast terahertz photoconductivity and near-field imaging of nanoscale inhomogeneities in multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons
item.description:
Author(s): Arvind Singh, Jan Kunc, Tinkara Troha, Hynek Němec, and Petr Kužel

We study broadband terahertz (THz) conductivity and ultrafast photoconductivity spectra in lithographically fabricated multilayer epitaxial graphene nanoribbons grown on C-face of 6H-SiC substrate. THz near-field spectroscopy reveals local conductivity variations across nanoscale structural inhomoge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115417] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

THz near-field spectroscopy reveals local conductivity variations across nanoscale structural inhomoge…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115417] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Microscopic phase-transition framework for gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen

Gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer WTe2 exhibits puzzling anomalies beyond the standard paradigm. Here, the authors develop a beyond-mean-field microscopic framework incorporating fermionic quasiparticles, bosonic Nambu-Goldstone phase modes, topological Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless excitations, and disorder to determine the superconducting gap, superfluid density, and phase transition. The theory explains the contrasting carrier-density dependence of the transition temperature in weakly and strongly disordered regimes and the sudden disappearance of superconducting fluctuations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100501] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen

Gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer WTe2 exhibits puzzling anomalies beyond the standard paradigm. Here, the authors develop a beyond-mean-field microscopic framework incorporating fermionic quasiparticles, bosonic Nambu-Goldstone phase modes, topological Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless excitations, and disorder to determine the superconducting gap, superfluid density, and phase transition. The theory explains the contrasting carrier-density dependence of the transition temperature in weakly and strongly disordered regimes and the sudden disappearance of superconducting fluctuations.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L100501] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic phase-transition framework for gate-tunable superconductivity in monolayer ${\mathrm{WTe}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: F. Yang, G. D. Zhao, Y. Shi, and L. Q. Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Topological signatures of magnetic phase transitions with Majorana fermions through local observables and quantum information
item.description:
Author(s): Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

The one-dimensional (1D) ${J}_{1}−{J}_{2}$ quantum spin model can be viewed as a strong-coupling analog of the Schrieffer-Su-Heeger model with two inequivalent alternating Ising couplings along the wire, associated to the physics of resonating valence bonds. Similar to the quantum Ising model, which…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121106] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

The one-dimensional (1D) J1−J2 quantum spin model can be viewed as a strong-coupling analog of the Schrieffer-Su-Heeger model with two inequivalent alternating Ising couplings along the wire, associated to the physics of resonating valence bonds. Similar to the quantum Ising model, which differently…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121106] Published Tue Mar 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Topological signatures of magnetic phase transitions with Majorana fermions through local observables and quantum information
item.dc:creator: Karyn Le Hur, Fan Yang, and Magali Korolev

item.title: Emergence of $p$-wave collinear magnetism in antiferromagnets with reflection-asymmetric magnetic motifs
item.description:
Author(s): Reynel Cárdenas, Valeria Quintana, Lin-Ding Yuan, and Carlos Mera Acosta

The classification of collinear magnetic phases in analogy with atomic orbitals and superconducting pairings has long lacked a collinear $p$-wave counterpart—systems exhibiting linear-in-$k$ spin splitting (SS) without net magnetization or spin-orbit coupling. Here, we resolve this gap by identifyin…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094431] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Reynel Cárdenas, Valeria Quintana, Lin-Ding Yuan, and Carlos Mera Acosta

The classification of collinear magnetic phases in analogy with atomic orbitals and superconducting pairings has long lacked a collinear p-wave counterpart—systems exhibiting linear-in-k spin splitting (SS) without net magnetization or spin-orbit coupling. Here, we resolve this gap by identifying a …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094431] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Roles of electron-magnon cross diffusion in unidirectional magnetoresistance of metallic magnetic bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Shashank Gupta and Steven S.-L. Zhang

Unidirectional magnetoresistance (UMR) in metallic bilayers arises from nonlinear spin-charge transport mediated by broken time-reversal and inversion symmetries, yet the role of magnons remains unsettled. We develop a theoretical framework that incorporates coupled electron-magnon dynamics, reveali…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094434] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shashank Gupta and Steven S.-L. Zhang

Unidirectional magnetoresistance (UMR) in metallic bilayers arises from nonlinear spin-charge transport mediated by broken time-reversal and inversion symmetries, yet the role of magnons remains unsettled. We develop a theoretical framework that incorporates coupled electron-magnon dynamics, reveali…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094434] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Localization-landscape generalized Mott-Berezinskiĭ formula
item.description:
Author(s): Gabriel Hayoun, Ilya A. Gruzberg, and Marcel Filoche

We introduce a conceptual reformulation of the Mott-Berezinskiĭ (MB) theory of low-frequency ac conductivity in disordered systems based on localization-landscape theory. Instead of assuming uniform localization and fixed hopping distances, transport is described through an effective potential whose…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104202] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

Author(s): Gabriel Hayoun, Ilya A. Gruzberg, and Marcel Filoche

We introduce a conceptual reformulation of the Mott-Berezinskiĭ (MB) theory of low-frequency ac conductivity in disordered systems based on localization-landscape theory. Instead of assuming uniform localization and fixed hopping distances, transport is described through an effective potential whose…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104202] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: General theory for the anomalous valley Hall effect in two-dimensional tetragonal crystals via static control
item.description:
Author(s): Xinyi Wang, Hongfu Zhou, Luyin Zhao, and Fujun Liu

The valley degree of freedom provides a promising route for functionalizing two-dimensional materials, yet a unified principle for realizing the anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) in tetragonal systems under static control has remained elusive. Here, we establish a general symmetry criterion for ac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104429] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinyi Wang, Hongfu Zhou, Luyin Zhao, and Fujun Liu

The valley degree of freedom provides a promising route for functionalizing two-dimensional materials, yet a unified principle for realizing the anomalous valley Hall effect (AVHE) in tetragonal systems under static control has remained elusive. Here, we establish a general symmetry criterion for ac…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104429] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Impact of the honeycomb spin lattice on topological magnons and edge states in ferromagnetic two-dimensional skyrmion crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Doried Ghader and Bilal Jabakhanji

Magnons have been intensively studied in two-dimensional (2D) ferromagnetic (FM) skyrmion crystals (SkXs) stabilized on Bravais lattices, particularly triangular and square lattices. In these systems, topological edge states (TESs) have been reported in higher-energy magnon gaps, while the first mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104430] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Doried Ghader and Bilal Jabakhanji

Magnons have been intensively studied in two-dimensional (2D) ferromagnetic (FM) skyrmion crystals (SkXs) stabilized on Bravais lattices, particularly triangular and square lattices. In these systems, topological edge states (TESs) have been reported in higher-energy magnon gaps, while the first mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104430] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Magnetic behavior of the $5{d}^{1}$ Re-based double perovskite ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{ZnReO}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Muhammad Maikudi Isah, Biswajit Dalal, Xun Kang, Dario Fiore Mosca, Ifeanyi John Onuorah, Valerio Scagnoli, Pietro Bonfà, Roberto De Renzi, Alexei A. Belik, Cesare Franchini, Kazunari Yamaura, and Samuele Sanna

The subtle interplay between spin-orbit coupling, exchange interactions, and cation ordering can lead to exotic magnetic states in transition-metal ions. We report a comprehensive study of the Re-based ($5{d}^{1}$) ordered double perovskite oxide ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{2}{\mathrm{ZnReO}}_{6}$ combining syn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104431] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

The subtle interplay between spin-orbit coupling, exchange interactions, and cation ordering can lead to exotic magnetic states in transition-metal ions. We report a comprehensive study of the Re-based (5d1) ordered double perovskite oxide Sr2ZnReO6 combining synchrotron x-ray diffraction (XRD), mag…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104431] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026

item.title: Thermally controlled flux avalanche dynamics in the bulk superconductor NbTi
item.description:
Author(s): I. Abaloszewa, V. V. Chabanenko, and A. Abaloszew

We report spatiotemporally resolved measurements of flux avalanche dynamics in bulk superconducting NbTi using high-speed magneto-optical imaging. Tracking individual avalanche events reveals propagation velocities of 15–25 m/s, which are orders of magnitude slower than the km/s speeds observed in t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104511] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Abaloszewa, V. V. Chabanenko, and A. Abaloszew

We report spatiotemporally resolved measurements of flux avalanche dynamics in bulk superconducting NbTi using high-speed magneto-optical imaging. Tracking individual avalanche events reveals propagation velocities of 15–25 m/s, which are orders of magnitude slower than the km/s speeds observed in t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104511] Published Mon Mar 16, 2026
